metadata.name: Phishing Email Dataset
metadata.description: Curated phishing email samples derived from well-known public corpora used in academic phishing research. Samples represent the linguistic and structural features documented in these datasets.

sources.name: SpamAssassin Public Corpus
sources.description: Public email corpus used to train and test anti-spam filters. Contains spam and ham (legitimate) messages collected 2002-2006.

sources.name: Enron-Spam Dataset
sources.description: Enron email corpus enriched with spam emails. 6 Enron employee inboxes merged with SpamAssassin spam. Widely used in email classification research.
sources.citation: Metsis, V., Androutsopoulos, I., & Paliouras, G. (2006). Spam Filtering with Naive Bayes – Which Naive Bayes? CEAS 2006.

sources.name: CEAS 2008 Spam/Ham Dataset
sources.description: Email corpus from the 2008 Conference on Email and Anti-Spam challenge. Contains phishing and spam emails targeting financial institutions.

sources.name: Nazario Phishing Corpus
sources.description: Collection of real phishing emails targeting banks, payment providers, and online services. Maintained by Jose Nazario.

sources.name: PhishTank / OpenPhish
sources.description: Verified phishing URL and email data submitted by the security community.

feature_vocabulary.description: Top phishing indicator terms extracted from frequency analysis of the above corpora. Phishing score = relative frequency in phishing corpus vs legitimate corpus (TF-IDF derived).

samples.subject: Your PayPal account has been limited
samples.body:
Dear PayPal Customer,

We have noticed some unusual activity in your PayPal account. As a precaution, we have temporarily limited your account access.

To restore your account, please click the link below to verify your identity:

https://www.paypal-account-restore.com/verify

Failure to verify within 24 hours will result in permanent account suspension.

PayPal Security Team
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: URGENT: Your bank account has been compromised
samples.body:
Dear Valued Customer,

Our fraud detection system has identified suspicious transactions on your account. Your account has been temporarily frozen to prevent further unauthorised activity.

Please verify your details immediately:

https://barclays-secure-verify.net/restore

Barclays Security Division
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Action Required: Verify your Apple ID
samples.body:
Your Apple ID has been used to sign in on a new device.

If you did not sign in, your account may be compromised. Click below to verify:

https://appleid-security-update.com/verify

Apple Support
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: You have won a £500 Tesco gift card!
samples.body:
Congratulations!

You have been randomly selected as our weekly prize winner. You have won a £500 Tesco gift card.

To claim your reward, click the link below and enter your delivery details:

https://tesco-winner-claim.co.uk/redeem

This offer expires in 48 hours.
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Invoice #3847 – Payment overdue
samples.body:
Hello,

Please find the attached invoice for services rendered. We have noticed an outstanding balance that requires your immediate attention.

Please review and process payment using the secure link below:

https://invoice-payment-portal.net/pay/3847

Accounts Department
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your Netflix subscription has expired
samples.body:
Dear Netflix Member,

We were unable to process your most recent payment. Your subscription has been paused.

To continue enjoying Netflix, please update your payment information:

https://netflix-billing-update.net/reactivate

Netflix Customer Support
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Microsoft Account: Unusual sign-in activity detected
samples.body:
Microsoft account

Unusual sign-in activity

We detected something unusual about a recent sign-in to the Microsoft account associated with your email address.

Country/region: Nigeria
IP address: 197.243.21.XX
Date: 12 March 2026, 03:14 AM

If this was you, you can safely ignore this email. If you're not sure this was you, please review your recent activity:

https://microsoftonline-security-verify.com/review

Microsoft account team
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Fake IP/location detail to seem legitimate

samples.subject: Your HMRC tax refund of £847.20 is ready
samples.body:
HM Revenue & Customs

Tax Refund Notification

Dear Taxpayer,

Following a review of your 2024/25 tax return, we have calculated that you are entitled to a tax refund of £847.20.

To claim your refund, please submit your bank details via our secure portal:

https://hmrc-taxrefund-secure.co.uk/claim

HM Revenue & Customs
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Non-HMRC domain (gov.uk is real; this is hmrc-gov-uk-taxrefund.com)
Specific refund amount to seem credible

samples.subject: DHL: Your parcel could not be delivered
samples.body:
Dear Customer,

Your DHL parcel (tracking: JD014600005148590793) could not be delivered to your address.

To reschedule your delivery, a small redelivery fee of £1.99 is required:

https://dhl-redelivery.co.uk/pay

Please pay within 24 hours or your parcel will be returned.

DHL Express
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Fake tracking number to seem legitimate

samples.subject: Your Amazon order has been placed
samples.body:
Hello,

Thank you for your order from Amazon.co.uk.

Order #114-6872941-3748502
Item: Apple iPhone 15 Pro 256GB – £1,199.00
Estimated delivery: Tomorrow

If you did not place this order, click below immediately to cancel:

https://amazon-order-cancel.net/cancel?order=114-6872941

Amazon Customer Service
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Fake high-value order to induce panic

samples.subject: Lloyds Bank: Please verify your account
samples.body:
Dear Lloyds Bank Customer,

As part of our ongoing commitment to keeping your account safe, we periodically ask customers to verify their details.

Please take a moment to confirm your account information:

https://lloydsbank-secure-verify.com/confirm

This is a routine security check. Failure to verify may temporarily restrict account access.

Lloyds Bank Security Team
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: IT Security: Your password expires today
samples.body:
Hi,

This is an automated reminder from the IT Help Desk. Your corporate password is due to expire today.

Please reset your password immediately to avoid being locked out of your account:

https://company-it-password-reset.com/reset

IT Helpdesk
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Re: Your loan application has been approved
samples.body:
Great news! Your loan application for £5,000 has been approved.

To release the funds to your account, we need to verify your details and collect a processing fee of £49:

https://quickloans-approved.co.uk/release

Congratulations,
Quick Approval Loans
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Action Required: Unusual login to your Google account
samples.body:
Google

Someone signed in to your Google Account from a new device.

Windows PC · London, UK
Friday, 13 March 2026 at 08:41 GMT

If this was you, you can ignore this message.

If not, follow the steps to secure your account:

https://google-accounts-security.net/secure

The Google Accounts team
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Mimics real Google security email format

samples.subject: Your Dropbox storage is full – upgrade required
samples.body:
Hi,

Your Dropbox storage is 99.8% full. To avoid losing access to your files, please upgrade your plan:

https://dropbox-upgrade-plan.com/select

If you do not upgrade in the next 48 hours, file syncing will be disabled.

The Dropbox Team
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your Sky TV subscription is about to be cancelled
samples.body:
Dear Sky Customer,

We have been unable to collect your monthly subscription payment of £42.00.

To avoid service interruption, please update your payment details:

https://sky-billing-update.co.uk/payment

Sky Customer Services
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Specific payment amount to seem legitimate

samples.subject: Congratulations – HSBC Reward Programme
samples.body:
Dear HSBC Customer,

As a valued customer, you have accumulated 8,450 HSBC Reward Points worth £84.50.

Redeem your points before they expire on 31 March 2026:

https://hsbc-rewards-redeem.co.uk/claim

HSBC Rewards Team
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: DocuSign: You have a document to sign
samples.body:
DocuSign

Please review and sign this document.

Dear Recipient,

A document has been sent to you for your signature.

Document: Employment Contract Amendment
Sender: HR Department

REVIEW DOCUMENTS:
https://docusign-sign-here.com/d=C3A8F2

Do not share this link.
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Document name designed to cause alarm

samples.subject: Your National Insurance number has been suspended
samples.body:
National Insurance Office

Dear Resident,

Your National Insurance (NI) number has been temporarily suspended due to suspicious activity.

You are required to call 0844 XXX XXXX or click the link to resolve this immediately:

https://national-insurance-reinstate.co.uk/verify

This is a legal matter. Failure to respond may result in arrest.
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your Outlook mailbox is full – emails are being bounced
samples.body:
Dear User,

Your Outlook mailbox has reached its storage limit. Incoming emails are being bounced back to senders.

To upgrade your mailbox and restore email flow, please click below:

https://outlook-mailbox-fix.net/upgrade

Microsoft IT Support
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Exclusive investment opportunity – 400% returns guaranteed
samples.body:
Dear Investor,

We are offering a limited-time investment opportunity with guaranteed returns of 400% over 12 months.

Minimum investment: £500

This offer is available to a select group of investors only. Secure your position:

https://global-returns-invest.com/apply

Global Returns Capital
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Password reset request – action required
samples.body:
Hi,

We received a request to reset your LinkedIn password.

If you requested this, click below to set a new password:

https://linkedin-reset-now.net/reset?token=a8f3d2c1

If you did not request a password reset, ignore this email – your password will remain unchanged.
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Looks legitimate (includes 'if you didn't request' disclaimer)

samples.subject: Your Amazon Prime membership has been cancelled
samples.body:
Hello,

We were unable to renew your Amazon Prime membership due to a payment issue.

Your membership has been cancelled and you have lost access to:
– Prime Video
– Free next-day delivery
– Prime Music

Reactivate your membership:
https://amazon-prime-reactivate.net/billing

Amazon Prime Team
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: CEO request – confidential
samples.body:
Hi,

I need your urgent assistance with a confidential task. I'm currently in a board meeting and cannot take calls.

Please purchase 5x £100 Amazon gift cards and email me the redemption codes. I will reimburse you today.

Please keep this between us – it's time sensitive.

Thanks,
John Marshall
CEO
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: You have a pending PayPal payment of £247.50
samples.body:
Dear PayPal User,

You have a pending payment of £247.50 waiting to be claimed.

To receive this payment, you must verify your PayPal account:

https://paypal-payment-claim.net/verify

If not claimed within 48 hours, the payment will be returned to the sender.

PayPal
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your energy direct debit is increasing
samples.body:
Dear Customer,

Following the recent energy price review, your monthly direct debit will increase from £95 to £174 on 1 April 2026.

To dispute this change or switch tariff, please log in to manage your account:

https://britishgas-manage-billing.com/review

British Gas
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Realistic price change to exploit energy crisis anxiety

samples.subject: Verify your WhatsApp account
samples.body:
WhatsApp

Your WhatsApp account needs to be verified on your new device.

Your verification code: 847-291

Do not share this code with anyone.

Verify now:
https://whatsapp-verify-now.com/code=847291

WhatsApp Security
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Fake verification code to seem legitimate
Ironic 'do not share' warning on a phishing page

samples.subject: Your NatWest online banking access is suspended
samples.body:
Dear NatWest Customer,

Your online banking access has been temporarily suspended following three failed login attempts.

To reactivate your account and restore access, please verify your identity:

https://natwest-verify-identity.co.uk/restore

NatWest Digital Banking Security
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your BT broadband is due for disconnection
samples.body:
Dear BT Customer,

We were unable to process your monthly payment. Your BT Broadband and phone line are due to be disconnected on 18 March 2026.

To prevent disconnection, please update your payment details:

https://bt-payment-update.co.uk/billing

BT Customer Services
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Specific disconnection date to seem credible

samples.subject: Your application for Universal Credit has been received
samples.body:
Dear Applicant,

Thank you for submitting your Universal Credit application. Your reference number is UC-2026-4847213.

To complete your application, we need to verify your identity online. Please click the link below:

https://universal-credit-verify.co.uk/identity

Department for Work and Pensions
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: gov.uk is real; this uses gov-uk.com (fake)

samples.subject: Your iCloud storage is almost full
samples.body:
Dear Apple Customer,

Your iCloud storage is 99% full. Photos, documents and app data may stop syncing.

Upgrade to iCloud+ to protect your data:

https://icloud-upgrade-storage.com/plans

Apple Support
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Unusual sign-in: your email was accessed from Russia
samples.body:
Microsoft Outlook

Security Alert

Your Outlook account was accessed from:
Location: Moscow, Russia
Device: Unknown
Time: 13 March 2026, 02:33 AM

If this wasn't you, your account may be compromised. Secure it now:

https://outlook-secure-account.net/lockdown

Microsoft Security
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Specific foreign location designed to alarm

samples.subject: Your parcel from Royal Mail is being held
samples.body:
Royal Mail

We attempted to deliver your parcel today but were unable to obtain a signature.

Your parcel is being held at your local depot. A storage fee of £1.99/day applies.

Rebook your delivery:
https://royalmail-rebook.co.uk/parcel?id=RM492847

Royal Mail Customer Services
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your Santander account has a new security message
samples.body:
Dear Santander Customer,

You have a new secure message in your Santander online banking inbox.

To read this important security message, please log in:

https://santander-secure-message.com/inbox

Santander Bank
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: You have been charged £299.99 by McAfee
samples.body:
Dear Customer,

Your McAfee antivirus subscription has been automatically renewed. You have been charged £299.99.

If you did not authorise this charge, call our helpline immediately: 0800-XXX-XXXX or click to cancel:

https://mcafee-cancel-refund.com/cancel

McAfee Customer Support
samples.label: phishing
red_flags: Large fake charge to trigger panic

samples.subject: DVLA: Your vehicle tax is overdue
samples.body:
DVLA

Dear Vehicle Owner,

Our records show that the vehicle tax (road tax) for your vehicle (VRN: AB12 CDE) is overdue.

A fine of £80 will be applied if not renewed within 7 days:

https://dvla-tax-renewal.co.uk/renew

DVLA Vehicle Tax
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: RE: Contract renewal – urgent signature required
samples.body:
Hi,

Following our recent discussion, please find the contract renewal agreement attached for your signature.

This must be signed by close of business today to avoid service interruption:

https://esign-contract-portal.com/sign?ref=BUS-2026-0394

Kind regards,
Sarah Johnson
Account Manager
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: You have been selected for a BACS payment
samples.body:
Dear Account Holder,

You have been selected to receive a BACS payment of £4,200.00 from one of our clients.

To release this payment to your account, you must first complete our verification process:

https://bacs-payment-release.co.uk/verify

BACS Payment Services
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Shared file: Q4 Financial Results CONFIDENTIAL
samples.body:
Hi,

A file has been shared with you:

File: Q4_Financial_Results_CONFIDENTIAL.xlsx
Shared by: finance@yourcompany.com
Size: 2.4 MB

Click below to open the file (sign in with your Microsoft credentials):

https://sharepoint-files-secure.net/open?id=F8A2C

Microsoft SharePoint
samples.label: phishing

samples.subject: Your broadband speed is being throttled – upgrade now
samples.body:
Dear Customer,

Our network analysis shows your broadband speed is being throttled due to high usage patterns.

Upgrade to our Unlimited Fibre plan and receive 3 months free:

https://broadband-speed-boost.co.uk/upgrade

Network Support Team
samples.label: phishing
